content: Thank YOU for your amazing help!
Thank you for your detailed list!
I'm not sure what I did differently on the second image that made the description less clear (should I have put "updated text messages at the start?)
I see!  I have updated the list according to your description.

Hello! There was a little mess-up with the RU team: Serhii Pidhorodetskyi and Kainoxis are the same team member, a name and a nickname. Please leave just Kainoxis and remove Serhii, so there wont be any doubling or phantom volunteers

Also, please, add another RU team member, Elandriah! She also contributed to the proofreading of the general terms.

Alright, **big** post incoming. I completely reworked Horla's kit, very few lines were not changed in some way.
I did this mostly as a proof of concept between other topics, to prove to myself I'm up to the task of tackling the game's text on this scale like I want to, and I'm pretty damn happy with the results.

Yes, Ace said that (a) changes like this aren't in the current scope of the volunteer project, (b) this kind of work is in need of being done on the CN side first, and (c) of all the things to rework, Awakeners kits have to be addressed *very carefully* because "people pay for them." However, she also said that the devs would be happy to have me create a summary of the changes I'd make, and potentially with a little translation back to CN, help inform the devs' decisions on how they want to structure the CN going forward.
So, if this rewrite is well received, I'll be moving forward with the project in hopes to have a decent amount of it done around the time the devs decide they want to touch these topics. I'll be committing these over time into my own fork of the project, and won't be making pull requests until I get the go-ahead. I'll also be trying to make semi-detailed write-ups about the decisions I end up making and why, in case it's helpful in the long run.

Without further ado, here's the file, and I'll follow this with a couple responses that include those changelogs and writeups.

- Changed "emotion/state" to **"Mood"**
  - Shorter word helps declutter walls of text to make kit easier to read.
  - ["Mood" connotation fits Horla's thematic identity as a writer better than "emotion".](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/mood#dictionary-entry-2)
  - It also appeared as "Mood" in the Talent description of "Writing with Feeling", so it appears to have been "Mood" at some point in development as well.

- Changed "Happiness" to **"Joy"**
  - Shorter word helps declutter walls of text to make kit easier to read.
  - "Joy" matches the in-game visual effect's "Joyeux" better than "Happiness".
  - It also appeared as "Joy" in the Awakener Trial stage description, so it appears to have been "Joy" at some point in development as well.

- Placed newlines (`\n`) between main card text and <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap text.
  - This structural change will help visually differentiate the card's primary effects from the Leap effects.
- Placed spaces (` `) at the end of Desc and BattleDesc card text.
  - Ideally, I'd like to end BattleDesc card text with (`\n`), so that affixes like <:warn:1408311425952387122>Exhaust and <:warn:1408311425952387122>Retain can be separated on a new line. Horla's kit is an exception; her Psalms have extra text that is appended when she's Roused, and that gets placed *before* the affixes.
  - This structural change allows affixes like <:warn:1408311425952387122>Retain to avoid concatenating with the last word of the card, while also conveniently allowing a seamless merge with the reformatted Rouse appends to create a proper sentence.
-# on that note...
- Placed newlines (`\n`) at the end of text appended to card BattleDesc(s) when Horla is Roused.
  - This structural change is intended to allow affixes like <:warn:1408311425952387122>Exhaust and <:warn:1408311425952387122>Retain to be displayed on a new line, separating them and other investigation-dependent affixes from the primary text of the card.
  - However, it may have the unintended consequence of pushing card text further up the card by creating an empty line. If this happens and the result is undesirable, these should be replaced with spaces (` `).

- Standardized all instances of each Mood word to use the provided color tags.
  - (i.e. `<D06CardKeyWordnu:Anger>` to display "`Anger`" in red)
  - This stylistic change is intended to build cohesion between effects and help break up walls of text into easier to digest chunks.
- Reordered the wording of some effects, including Exalt "Resplendent Chapters" to consistently follow the order of Psalms
  - The order of Psalms in this case being the order in which they are displayed as cards in Awakener menus: "Snarl Psalm" (Strike, Anger); "Elegy Psalm" (Defense, Grief); "Carol Psalm" (Skill1, Joy); "Dirge Psalm" (Skill2, Fear).
  - Consistently presenting Mood effects in `Anger`-`Grief`-`Joy`-`Fear` order can help players find patterns between effects and increase comprehension during gameplay.

- Removed redundant explanation of Horla's mechanics from her Talent "Writing with Feeling".
  - The half-baked explanation of what Psalms do in regards to Moods and vice versa was entirely redundant as it's explained much better in other places, and only served to obscure the actual relevant text of the talent (that I completely missed in-game), that being the <:deathresistance:1408324276540407870>Death Resistance-related buff.

- Significantly retemplated tooltip and affix descriptions of Moods to be more clear at a glance about what is buffed
  - This structural change includes the use of `\n` and `•` to make bulleted lists.
- Reworded Mood descriptions to clarify that only the effects caused BY Awakeners are buffed.
  - Previously, it was unclear what effects are and aren't buffed, and this can make it at least a little more clear.

Syntax changes:

- In MOST if not all cases:
  - Changed `Happiness` to `Joy`
  - Changed `emotion`/`state` to `Mood`
  - Changed `restore` to `recover`
  - Changed `additional` to `extra`
  - Changed `obtain` to `gain` or `grant`
  - Changed `apply` to `inflict` in the context of inflicting debuffs on enemies

- Re-templated STR reduction effects to "inflict STR▼"
  - This one is one of my biggest pet peeves. The way that STR reduction appears to work in this game is that it's literally a debuff called <:strdown:1408311306058203236>STR▼. Any effect that applies this debuff should describe it as doing exactly that.

Excerpts and examples of major changes made:
*note: these will be presented without rich text tags, and with \n as true line breaks, for ease of reading.*
*any instances of Psalm card names and Mood names placed in `code` here will be displayed in their corresponding colors in-game.*
Hopefully putting these up front and center can help showcase some of the structural and stylistic changes and give examples of my writing style.

## - Awakener Trial Description:
> Horla's 4 Command cards are "Psalms"; triggering <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap with a Psalm will activate one of four corresponding "Moods" (`Anger`, `Grief`, `Joy`, and `Fear`), mutually exclusive buff states that grant all Awakeners' effects different bonuses depending on which Mood is active.
> Horla's Exalt causes 4 different effects, and each one will also be individually buffed by its corresponding Mood. Horla's Rouse grants her "Metaphors" over time, stacking buffs that give bonuses to Psalms that trigger <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap.
> In this Trial, you will bring Horla and Lotan into battle at Level 50, Skill Level 5, and Enlightenment Level 3. Enemies will receive a buff on their first turn.

## - "Snarl Psalm" outside-battle Card Description:
> Deal `Arg1` DMG. Horla gains `Arg3` Aliemus.
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap: Switch Mood to "`Anger`". For each `Anger` Metaphor consumed, deal an extra 2 instances of DMG. 

## - "Dirge Psalm" in-battle Card Text, with E2:
> Gain `Arg1` <:str:1408311379932221450>STR and inflict `Arg2` stacks of <:str:1408311379932221450>STR▼ on all enemies.
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap: Switch Mood to "`Fear`" <:warn:1408311425952387122>Retain.
- when Roused:
> Gain `Arg1` <:str:1408311379932221450>STR and inflict `Arg2` stacks of <:str:1408311379932221450>STR▼ on all enemies.
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap: Switch Mood to "`Fear`" and consume `(N)` `Fear` Metaphor(s) to gain an extra `(X)` <:str:1408311379932221450>STR.
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Retain.

## - Over-Exaltation "Resplendent Chapters"
> Temporary Crit. Rate & Crit. DMG +`Arg4`%. Recover `Arg1` HP. Grant all other Awakeners `Arg3` Aliemus. Gain `Arg2` <:str:1408311379932221450>STR. Different Moods increase this Exalt's corresponding effects by 600%. Gain 3 stacks of <:warn:1408311425952387122>Universal Metaphor; they will be treated as Metaphors of any Mood, and be consumed by the Leap of any "Psalm". 
-# *standard Exalt and E3 Exalt are identical to this, up to and including the corresponding "by X%".*

## - Talent "Writing with Feeling" Description:
> "Snarl Psalm" is considered to be a Basic Strike card; "Elegy Psalm" is considered to be a Basic Defense card.
> For every 1% of <:deathresistance:1408324276540407870>Death Resistance provided by Horla, the buffs that Moods have on Awakeners' effects is increased by 0.1%.

## - "Metaphor" Card Description Tooltip:
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Metaphor
> Each Mood's Metaphor has a maximum of 3 stacks. When a Psalm card triggers <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap, it consumes all of its Mood's Metaphors to enhance its effects:
> "`Snarl Psalm`" consumes `Anger` Metaphors to deal extra instances of DMG.
> "`Elegy Psalm`" consumes `Grief` Metaphors to recover HP.
> "`Carol Psalm`" consumes `Joy` Metaphors to grant extra Aliemus.
> "`Dirge Psalm`" consumes `Fear` Metaphors to gain extra <:str:1408311379932221450>STR.

**I'd like to compare the following examples directly with their current in-game text.**

## "Metaphor" Combat Status Tooltip:
- Current:
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Metaphor
> Possessing the metaphor `"Anger"` `Arg2` layer: "Snarl Psalm" triggers Leap and deals additional Damage.
> Possessing the metaphor `"Sorrow"` `Arg3` layer: "Elegy Psalm" triggers Leap and restores additional Life.
> Possessing the metaphor `"Happiness"` `Arg1` layer: "Carol Psalm" triggers Leap and gives additional Aliemus to Awakeners.
> Possessing the metaphor `"Fear"` `Arg4` layer: "Dirge Psalm" triggers Leap and gives additional STR.
- Revised:
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Metaphor
> When a "Psalm" card triggers <:warn:1408311425952387122>Leap and switches Moods, it consumes all of that Mood's Metaphor stacks to enhance the card's effects.
> Current `Anger` Metaphor stacks: `Arg2`.
> Current `Grief` Metaphor stacks: `Arg3`.
> Current `Joy` Metaphor stacks: `Arg1`.
> Current `Fear` Metaphor stacks: `Arg4`.

I decided here not to repeat the *(in 2/4 cases literally incorrect)* descriptions of what each Psalm does with each Metaphor, as that's already explained much better by the actual Psalm cards themselves, as well as by both the Metaphor tooltip and the specific Mood tooltip that's brought up when selecting a Psalm card. Keeping this list short and simple while still supplying a general reminder of what they're for is all this tooltip should do.

## "Grief" Mood Tooltips
- Current Card Description Tooltip:
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>"Grief"
> All Awakeners' Shield and HP Recovery effects, as well as the <:str:1408311379932221450>STR debuff effect they cause, are increased by `Arg1`%. The recovery effect of Resplendent Chapters is also increased.
- Revised Card Description Tooltip:
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Mood: "`Grief`"
> Shield gain, HP recovery, and <:str:1408311379932221450>STR▼ infliction caused by all Awakeners' effects increases by `Arg1`%. "Resplendent Chapters" recovers more HP.

- Current Combat Status Tooltip:
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>Grief
> All Awakeners' Shield and HP Recovery effects, as well as the <:str:1408311379932221450>STR reduction effect, are increased by `Arg1`%. Increase the HP Recovery of "Resplendent Chapters".
- Revised Combat Status Tooltip:
> <:warn:1408311425952387122>`Grief`
> The following effects caused by all Awakeners are buffed by `Arg1`%:
> • Shield Gained
> • HP Recovered
> • <:str:1408311379932221450>STR▼ Inflicted
> "Resplendent Chapters" recovers more HP.

This probably isn't a fully exhaustive list of the changes but it should summarize them well enough. I provided the file up at the OP for anyone who wants to check my work.
<@272394963642220545> <@88644018673582080> <@364412673275723779> <@123141228731564035> Would love to field y'all's opinions.

[Click here to jump quickly to the top *(this is definitely not a rickroll)*](https://discord.com/channels/1402537664619479100/1415196228219834389/1415196228219834389)

1) (Snarl/Elergy Psalm) IIRC I think we concluded that Vulnerability/Weakness is stacks and not turns
2) (Elegy Psalm/Resplendent Chapters) I'm pretty sure we haven't come to the consensus that Recover is the right term for healing
3) (Rouse) not a fan of "process" would rather see "Repeat this effect"
4) (Overexalt) I still would like OE to specifically specify that Universal Metaphor is consumed before mood specific Metaphor
5) for passive, is there a reason we need to specify "Basic Strike" instead of just "Strike"? IIRC Strike is effectively its own Keyword descriptor for a number of characters (IE Lotan and her 2/3 cost cards and Exalt). I'd rather word it like "Snarl Psalm is also considered a Strike Card. Elegy Psalm is also considered a Defense card."
6) mood state: I'm not a big fan of the phrasing "The following effects caused by all Awakeners are buffed by". I think there's a more elegant way to write this.
7) minor nitpicks: Anger should be "grants more Temp Crit Rate/DMG" not give
Joy: Command Cards should be capitalized IIRC
second instance of anger should be "Resplendant Chapters grants increased (more?) Crit Rate & Crit DMG

- *Mood* : I like Emotion since it has the right flavor, but I see your point, let's see what the kit looks like with it
- *Joy* ✅
- \n before "Leap" : sure
- space at the end : what ?
It would probably be more coherent to just have a space at the beginning of the metaphor effects (49640-49643), and would avoid any alignment mishaps. I'm against having unnecessary characters in the main description.
- rouse effects' \n : This is Leap text, so technically affix text. A space is fine in my opinion, but if it doesn't take too much space on the card a newline could be fine too
- color ✅
- Exalt/OE : the increased effect part is still not clear enough to me, I'd suggest changing "*Different Moods increase this Exalt's corresponding effects by 600%*" to `The effect associated with the current Mood is increased 600%` or generally any formulation that uses the **singular** to talk about the one current Mood instead of Moods in general.
The consistent ordering is 👌
- talent : ❌ I do not fear redundancy. The part of the talent that explains that her strike & def are treated as psalms is important. Having all 4 cards count as something is quite the unique property. I'd recommend a rewrite to convey that information at least (also her atk/def are considered psalms, not the other way around).
- obtain to gain or grant : having the same verb for aliemus & shield especially in the same card text feels extremely wrong.
- inflict STR▼: Horla does not inflict  STR▼, she inflicts TEMPORARY  STR▼, which is completely different. It is a debuff that works the same and you can absolutely use the debuff's formatting, but you should ABSOLUTELY NEVER lose the nuance that this is temporary. Permanent str down exists (for bosses, mobs & awakeners alike), so you should always convey the duration & the temporary nature of this reduction. **Do not send the changes without this info**.

I second Dredge on :
3) "process" -> "effect"
4) though ideally pushed to a tooltip about metaphor/universal metaphor, this is info that should be somewhere
6) not a fan either, a simple `for all Awakeners` conveys the info in a clean & comprehensible manner.

And lastly I really liked when Horla's card names had the emotion in them, because it was peak gameplay experience.
I fully understand how the current situation brings in more flavor (*and is still pretty good for a player that knows Horla*), but I'd like to get that out there.

> 1) (Snarl/Elergy Psalm) IIRC I think we concluded that Vulnerability/Weakness is stacks and not turns
I still believe it's important to use terminology that shortcuts understandability to the player when available. Any stacking status that loses exactly 1 stack at the end of each turn should use "turns" and not "stacks". "Stacks" is not a strict requirement for the wording of these effects; we don't "gain 36 stacks of shield". **Hardline, I'm not moving on this issue.**

> 2) (Elegy Psalm/Resplendent Chapters) I'm pretty sure we haven't come to the consensus that Recover is the right term for healing
I still stand by my statements on Recover vs Restore/Heal/Gain that I made in <#1408863441422385289>. My last statement, which has had no further discussion to dispute it, was:
> Agreed on Arithmetica, we'll use Gain for that. I still disagree with the usage of Gain for HP though, as it deserves special treatment. Restore is being specialized for something else, so that leaves us with Recover or Heal.
> I continue to stand on my opinion that this is a game more focused on fanciful language as opposed to mechanics-first language, so the only reasonable usage of "Heal" being as a keyword instead of as part of a sentence clashes with the overall aim of my vision for the project, so I'm going to move forward on using Recover for HP until I run into a significant obstacle.
**Soft disagree;** I could be moved on this issue with a significantly better idea that works better in every context than "Recover" would.

> 3) (Rouse) not a fan of "process" would rather see "Repeat this effect"
"Effect" is to be used in a very particular way that's hard for me to describe perfectly. "Effect" here would imply that the *effect* the card had on the gamestate would be repeated; "Process" implies that the *calculation* would be repeated. **Disagree;** higher authority would need to chime in for me to change my mind here, as it would have implications on the way I write other things.

> 4) (Overexalt) I still would like OE to specifically specify that Universal Metaphor is consumed before mood specific Metaphor
From what I understand with testing, any time Metaphors are consumed, ALL of a Mood's Metaphors are consumed, so there is no "before" to specify here. **Soft disagree;** contradictory evidence can move me on this issue.

> 5) for passive, is there a reason we need to specify "Basic Strike" instead of just "Strike"? IIRC Strike is effectively its own Keyword descriptor for a number of characters (IE Lotan and her 2/3 cost cards and Exalt).
I've seen "Basic Strike" somewhere else before, but can't recall where. It seems important in some backend functionality for the game to be able to distinguish specifically what is and isn't, in any other kit, "Strike" and "Defense". **Soft agree;** this could definitely be worded better if I understood the game better.
-# *sorta related: the game seems to be conflicted (basic, initial, innate, inherent, etc) on what to call specifically the initial 4 cards that an Awakener has built in to their kit. I'm leaning towards referring to these as "Skill Cards" or "Skills", of which Snarl and Elegy Psalm would be a Strike Skill and Defense Skill.*

> 6) mood state: I'm not a big fan of the phrasing "The following effects caused by all Awakeners are buffed by". I think there's a more elegant way to write this.
**Soft agree;** sadly I didn't cook up anything better, but it's important it communicates something specific, see my response to Goz below.

> Joy: Command Cards should be capitalized IIRC
**Hard agree;** I've been fighting myself over the capitalization of card types and honestly I think you're right. My initial thought was "Cards" should have `C` if it's standalone, but have `c` if preceded by a card type (like Command card or Symptom card) but I think it won't be that jarring to have it all capitalized all the time. **I'll go back and edit the events file today.**

> space at the end : what ?
> It would probably be more coherent to just have a space at the beginning of the metaphor effects (49640-49643), and would avoid any alignment mishaps. I'm against having unnecessary characters in the main description.
Like I said, ideally we should have either a space or newline (after sleeping on it, leaning towards space) at the end of **ALL** card text, AND at the end of **ALL** affix text, to allow text rendering to create a new line and prevent long chains of run-on appended text.
Spaces at the beginning of a string risk getting culled when imported and exported through different filetypes and programs, so they should be at the end of strings; they're also much less visible at the end than at the beginning.
Horla's situation is unique in that it's very common (E2) for *all* of her cards to have "<:warn:1408311425952387122>﻿Retain.", and the Rouse affixes are placed after the main text and before the "<:warn:1408311425952387122>﻿Retain." text. **Soft disagree;** I would have to decide to change the way I templated the affixes entirely to move on this, but I'm not terribly opposed to doing that, I just like it this way
﻿
> Exalt/OE : the increased effect part is still not clear enough to me, I'd suggest changing "*Different Moods increase this Exalt's corresponding effects by 600%*" to `The effect associated with the current Mood is increased 600%` or generally any formulation that uses the **singular** to talk about the one current Mood instead of Moods in general.
The decision hidden here is a complex one, that I don't have enough experience with templating this game's effects to have a solid decision on yet; that being how we want to display to the player that a precalculated value *has already been* increased by another value. In reality, this is the ⁠The STR Bonus Discussion﻿ problem all over again. **Soft agree;** I'll go with your suggestion but may later down the line revisit this issue if I have a clear vision for this kind of problem.
﻿
> talent : ❌﻿ I do not fear redundancy. The part of the talent that explains that her strike & def are treated as psalms is important. Having all 4 cards count as something is quite the unique property. I'd recommend a rewrite to convey that information at least (also her atk/def are considered psalms, not the other way around).
I rewrote the talent specifically to emphasize that her first 2 Psalms are Strike/Defense; the redundancy I culled was the half-baked explanation of how leaping psalms triggers moods and moods have effects on psalms, which was *almost half* of the talent description, pushing the important part (D-Resist buff) so far down I failed to recognize it was there until I went to edit this cell in excel.
"Strike" and "Defense" are two things simultaneously: a card name, and a property of a card. Nearly ALL cards that are "Strikes" are called "Strike", but there are exceptions; Snarl Psalm being one of them. "Psalm" is *not* a property of a card in the same way; "Psalm" specifically refers to Horla's 4 Skill cards, all of which contain "Psalm" in the name.
If another effect completely different from Horla begins to refer to cards as being "considered Psalms", I'll rewrite this. As it stands though, it's unique to Horla's kit, and the talent should explain that something unique to her kit *is considered to be* something everything else references, not the other way around. **Hardline, not moving.**
﻿
> obtain to gain or grant : having the same verb for aliemus & shield especially in the same card text feels extremely wrong.
Not really sure how to dispute this other than to say I don't really understand why you feel that way or how I could fix that. A lot of thought has been put into my verb choices here, and this doesn't really help construct a better framework than what I picked. **Hard disagree;** *...sorry?*
﻿
> inflict STR▼: Horla does not inflict  STR▼, she inflicts TEMPORARY  STR▼
Correct! Solid proofreading, thanks! Fixed.

> 3) "process" -> "effect"
> 4) though ideally pushed to a tooltip about metaphor/universal metaphor, this is info that should be somewhere
see my response to Dredge above.
﻿
> 6) not a fan either, a simple `for all Awakeners` conveys the info in a clean & comprehensible manner.
**HARD disagree;** through my playtesting of Horla's kit so far, I've come to the understanding that the way to know what is and isn't increased by Mood is to see if specifically *an Awakener* is *causing* the effect to happen. MUCH more playtesting, including methodically writing down literally every single action that can be taken that does and doesn't get buffed, would be required for the evidence needed for me to move on this issue. I spent a good four hours on this line alone, and while it's not pretty, any other wording I could possibly come up with risked misleading the player into thinking something would end up buffed when it isn't.
﻿
MTG has a similar problem that is informing my decision here; the difference between Doubling Season's `"If an effect would put one or more counters on a permanent(...)"`, Vorinclex's `If you would put one or more counters on a permanent(...)` and Branching Evolution's `If one or more (counters) would be put on a creature(...)` is subtle enough for a lot of players to think they all do the same thing, but the rigidity of Magic's rules text and underlying framework means there are many cases where one of these effects applies but the others don't. These things need to be *as **extremely clear** as the language we're given allows for* to avoid confusion.
<@272394963642220545> in regards to the stacks vs turns and heal vs recover vs whatever can we just @ ace and have her give us the hardline stance so we can put the nail on this argument
I disagree on effect vs process on your argument, the effect of getting mood is a repeated game state
I can write up a clean proposal to sum up the discussion and ping her later today. <:BennettThumbsUp:773345984474120202>

and sadly I can't personally test if her OE description in game is accurate because I am not wasting 4 universal dupes or 3 store dupes + $100 just to check if the in game text is actually how it works
as in "does it use 3 stacks max" or "does it use up both stacks + universal stacks"
It uses all stacks.
sure then whatever
It is possible to deal 13 instances of DMG with snarl psalm
also side note I really don't like how the game words the mood boost to exalt
it reads so badly to me
I just left it as is because that's a can of worms I am not smart enough to rewrite
but "mood coresponding" or whatever just feels like word vomit

My concern is this:
*(not at my PC right now and about to take a nap so I'm paraphrasing)*
As a framework-level thinker,
I read "Gain 1 Metaphor of each inactive Mood. Repeat this effect at the start of each turn."
I think "okay, so I'm in anger right now, so I'll get one of everything but anger every turn."
I read "Gain 1 Metaphor of each inactive Mood. Repeat this process at the start of each turn."
I think "okay, I'm in anger right now, so I'll get one of everything but anger, and next turn, I'll get one of everything but what I'm in at the time."
I think this would need to be run by some random people who don't know Horla's kit to see if this actually makes sense because process doesn't seem as clear to me as it is to you

I've been mostly hesitant to talk in public about any of the work I'm doing here cus I don't know what is and isn't supposed to be in the NDA we were supposed to sign, but for sure I'd like more opinions than just the folks here.
vulnerability/weakness need to be stacks, not turns
turns is incorrect wording and should not (personal opinion: **cannot**) be used
I admire the intent behind using turns for clarity but they are stacks, not turns
there is content where multiple stacks are removed per turn so using turns in that context would be directly incorrect and very misleading

the Japanese server removes 3 stacks per turn as well and reworks the way vul/weakness work so there is also very clear intent from a dev side for vul/weakness to be redone, which could be applicable for future rulesets or game content (new StS mode, for example, or a potential a3 or special event ruleset down the line)
turns just is not a very good term to use because of that tbh
I haven't looked into JP servers but that's crazy lol

regarding basic strike vs strike - there does need to be a differentiation between innate command cards and cards that are classified as strike/defense, so this is good. That said, I dislike the word "skills" for this and strongly recommend against it, because there is differentiation between strike/def cards and non-strike/def cards multiple places throughout the game, so using strike skill and defense skill risks causing confusion between the two. Also, just from personal experience, it's very common in the community to refer to non-strike/def character cards as skill cards, so you risk causing community confusion as well when people talk about skill cards or mention someone's skill1 or skill2, etc

I'm not saying that this change shouldn't be made, I'm just disagreeing with the use of the word skills. I haven't put any thought into an alternative but I'd much rather a different term be used

also, while I have no skin in the game with regards to process vs effect, I would advise more consideration and careful treading about enforcing terminology logic that other people don't understand and which is not clearly outlined by the game rules. This game doesn't have a defined game process/ruling order like how MTG has the stack and how yugioh has the chain, so trying to directly carry that over into this game is just going to miss the mark for the vast overwhelming majority of players who are not familiar with TCG rulesets/design. 

Yes, it'd make it easier for you when writing to classify and organize the effects in your head, but you cannot realistically expect other people to 1) understand the differentiation between process and effect without it being explained in the game rules and 2) abide by your personal wording structure when rewriting cards to match your vision of the game rather than the existing state of the game.

That said, I'm not saying you shouldn't make that differentiation in your head yourself if it helps you, I'm saying this should not be a sticking point nor should there be pushback about other people writing it differently since this is not an established game mechanic - there is going to be inconsistency and we need to accept that that is okay, because the original game design has inconsistencies. Attempting to hard enforce a certain framework or structure *before the actual game itself has one* is just going to cause friction between community members and additional work down the line if or when the devs do implement specific keywords and wording structures. In short, I'm just saying this isn't a good hill to die on, imo if people prefer effect to process, just go with it for now and you can always reconsider reworking it down the line if the specific need arises

Light has actually gone into detail about the JP server design/development, he's pretty clear in that the dev team considers some choices to be mistakes and are using the JP server to rectify that: https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1407352797091069962/1411291027846725653
I'm pretty sure the current implementation of vul/weakness is one of them, which is why it's so different on JP

he also does specifically say in that post that some of those changes would be ported back over into global later on in some way, which is why I'm very keen on *not* using turns because there's a very real possibility ours gets reworked/redone in some form, whether it be future content only or retroactive

the current implementation of vul/weakness on global servers is indeed far too strong, the values are too high and vul/weakness application ends up being mandatory requirements for every serious endgame team which restricts teambuilding and flexibility in a lot of ways

but also this isn't really something that's viable to change for us because it would require insanely wide sweeping changes as a whole which would seriously upend the state of the game and make players very upset, but redesigning those changes on a new from-scratch server and/or implementing them for future stuff is a lot more reasonable imo
tbh I like it a lot and think it's really neat, basically vul/weakness start at much lower values but every stack of it increases the intensity, and then every turn loses 3 stacks
so baseline vul/weakness is significantly weaker, but investing heavily into it actually gives you higher returns, so it's both less mandatory but also more rewarding for dedicating into it

but yeah because of the stack count it would require basically going through and reworking every weakness/vul application in the game + enlightens + relic effects to customize the number of stacks each one gives to balance it around the new system which is like....yeah that's not worth it

I can definitely understand if later down the line they decide to make Weak/Vuln not boolean on/off at a specific multiplier, they seem to already be experimenting with that with the "Blighten/Fragile Enhance" thing.
Stacks it is, then.

I'm definitely not directly enforcing logic from other games, that would be really dumb. The framework is different so the terms need to be different.

I also recognize that I should be a lot more relaxed when it comes to the semantics of things like the process/effect thing, it's not a fundamentally established mechanic so I have to accept that it's fine for some text to be confusing on occasion, because that's inevitable.
